$comment: DESIGN.md §20.3.1 point 6: a manifest may exclude a column/field from comparison for a specific pass only by citing a §21 caveat id. Second manifest in the corpus (after p10.manifest.json/C25) — this one exercises C24 as originally envisioned: a pure value skew, field-exemptable.

exemptions.reason: After the debuggee's own ROLLBACK exits a doomed transaction, @@TRANCOUNT stays faithful to native (0) only until the first statement requiring rollback protection. From that statement onward it reads +1 vs native, because the debugger's safety net (§16) re-opens its own transaction right before the write (deferred resurrection, A9), where native has nothing open (autocommit). Expected values: native=0, debugger=1. Documented, by-design divergence — see DESIGN.md §21 row C24.
